description:
To properly set the owner of /var/log/apt/*, run the command: 
$ sudo chown root /var/log/apt/*
rationale:
The <tt>/var/log/apt</tt> directory contains information about APT
and should only be accessed by authorized personnel.
ocil_clause: /var/log/apt/* does not have an owner of root
ocil:
To check the ownership of /var/log/apt/*, run the command: 
$ ls -lL /var/log/apt/*
If properly configured, the output should indicate the following owner: root
fixtext:
To properly set the owner of /var/log/apt/*, run the command: 
$ sudo chown root /var/log/apt/*
srg_requirement: The Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 /var/log/apt/* file must be owned by root.
title: Verify Ownership of Files in /var/log/apt
